mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides a seamless local development environment for building and testing containerized applications using Docker and Kubernetes. It is ideal for developers who want to run Kubernetes clusters locally without the overhead of managing a full-fledged cloud environment. Key trade-offs include limited scalability compared to cloud solutions and reliance on macOS.

steps.title: Download Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step installs Docker Desktop, which includes Kubernetes support.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

```

> Download the latest version of Docker Desktop for Mac.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description:
Run the downloaded installer to set up Docker Desktop on your Mac.

```bash

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

drag Docker to Applications

open /Applications/Docker.app

```

> Replace with the actual downloaded file name if different.
> This installs Docker Desktop.
> Launch Docker Desktop.

steps.title: Enable Kubernetes
steps.description:
Configure Docker Desktop to enable the Kubernetes feature.

```bash

open /Applications/Docker.app

click on 'Preferences' > 'Kubernetes'

```

> Open Docker Desktop.
> Enable the Kubernetes checkbox and click 'Apply & Restart'.

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description:
Check that Kubernetes is running properly on Docker Desktop.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see a node in 'Ready' state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description:
This will remove Docker Desktop and all associated Kubernetes resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

open /Applications

rm -rf Docker.app

```

> Navigate to Applications folder.
> This removes Docker Desktop.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description:
Backup your Docker images and containers, then upgrade Docker Desktop to the latest version.

```bash

docker save -o ~/docker-images.tar $(docker images -q)

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all Docker images.
> Download the latest version.
> Run the installer to upgrade.
> Verify nodes are in 'Ready' state after upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes returns 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes may not be enabled or running.

**Fix:**
Open Docker Desktop, go to Preferences > Kubernetes, enable it, and restart Docker.

troubleshooting.title: Docker Desktop fails to start
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient system resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase CPU and memory allocation in Docker Desktop Preferences.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl command not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'brew install kubectl' or ensure Docker Desktop is properly installed.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes cluster is not healthy
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check resource allocation in Docker Desktop Preferences and ensure Kubernetes is configured correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Improved Kubernetes integration and updated to Kubernetes v1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing at least one node in 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Mac meets the minimum system requirements for Docker Desktop.
